name: Chicken Thigh

disambiguationNotes: Do not substitute raw chicken thigh values.
Do not substitute skin-on, bone-in, breaded, fried, or marinated product values.
This draft is intentionally a cooked meat-only reference entry and should not be mistaken for the project's raw default meat baseline.

secondarySources.note: Better identity match found in sourcing pass: cooked, roasted, thigh meat only. This is materially closer to the current file target than the earlier raw meat-and-skin row.

changeNotes: Production draft now uses a better USDA anchor: fdcId 172388 / ndbNumber 5094.
This row is a materially better identity match because it covers thigh meat only, cooked, roasted.
The file wording has been tightened to meat-only roasted thigh so the entry no longer overclaims a skinless-specific lock that the row does not state explicitly.
Editorial non-nutrition context from the sample file was removed from scoring emphasis.

header.notes: Macros now align to USDA fdc:172388 for chicken thigh meat only, cooked, roasted.

protein_g.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
protein_g.note: Updated to the better-matching cooked roasted thigh meat-only USDA row.

fat_g.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
fat_g.note: Updated to the better-matching cooked roasted thigh meat-only USDA row.

kcal.note: Updated to the better-matching cooked roasted thigh meat-only USDA row.

omega3_mg.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
omega3_mg.note: Adjusted to a conservative ~13 mg total omega-3-equivalent from the attached cooked roasted thigh fatty-acid rows.

essential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

nonessential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

collagen_g.note: Displayed as N/A for now. This metric remains part of the final display schema, but the project does not yet have a defensible sourced method for collagen_g on this exact chicken-thigh identity.

bioavailability_percent.note: Displayed as N/A for now. This metric remains part of the final display schema, but the project does not yet have a defensible sourced method for this exact chicken-thigh identity.

pros.title: protein quality is one of the clearest strengths
pros.explanation: The amino-acid profile and general digestibility make chicken thigh's protein story stronger in practice than a label-only macro read suggests.

evidence.sourceName: animal-protein quality literature; exact citation still to be attached
evidence.citationNote: Well-supported directionally, but the exact citation bundle is still missing from the file.

pros.title: brings useful B12 and zinc alongside protein
pros.explanation: That gives it more nutritional substance than a pure protein-only framing.

evidence.sourceName: derived from vitamin_b12_dv and zinc_dv metrics
evidence.citationNote: Directly supported by the current draft nutrient panel.

pros.title: protein density can support satiety
pros.explanation: Useful context, but broad enough that it should stay modestly weighted.

evidence.sourceName: editorial judgment informed by protein-rich-food pattern

cons.title: saturated fat and cholesterol are not trivial
cons.explanation: Chicken thigh is not extreme, but this cut still carries a meaningful animal-fat caveat compared with leaner protein options.

evidence.sourceName: derived from saturated_fat_g and cholesterol_mg metrics
evidence.citationNote: Directly supported by the nutrient profile.

cons.title: preparation can change the health story a lot
cons.explanation: Breaded, fried, or sauce-heavy versions are a different food outcome from plain roasted thigh meat.

evidence.sourceName: identity-lock logic plus general food-preparation reality
evidence.citationNote: Project-valid caution; still not a substitute for product-specific sourcing if processed versions are added later.

cons.title: raw handling risk exists upstream of the final cooked food
cons.explanation: Worth acknowledging as practical context, but not a major nutrition-scoring argument.

primaryRemainingBlockers: Create a separate raw chicken-thigh canonical entry if the project wants a default wholefood meat baseline, rather than drifting this cooked entry.
Keep this entry explicitly scoped to roasted thigh meat only and do not relabel it back to skinless unless a row explicitly supports that wording.
Keep collagen_g and bioavailability_percent as explicit unsupported / N/A states unless a defensible method is adopted for this exact identity.

sourceNotes: Draft production-lane entry for roasted chicken thigh meat only.
The current USDA anchor is fdcId 172388 / ndbNumber 5094 for thigh meat only, cooked, roasted; that is the right row for the current attached nutrient panel.
This draft is now honest about being a cooked meat-only reference entry rather than pretending to be the raw-default meat baseline.
This draft intentionally strips out the sample file's non-nutrition ethical context from score-driving emphasis.
